zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John de Walton於大約1316年出生在英國艾可維文時，他的父親Sir Thomas Walton25歲，母親Elizabeth Aspall the Elder24歲。他於1339年與Margaret de Sutton在英國英格蘭艾可結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1393年在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾格亞特 斯陶頓享年78歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John de Walton于大约1316年出生在英国艾可維文时，他的父亲Sir Thomas Walton25岁，母亲Elizabeth Aspall the Elder24岁。他于1339年与Margaret de Sutton在英国英格兰艾可结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1393年在英国英格兰坎布利其爾格亞特 斯陶頓享年78岁。

nameMeanings.name: Walton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Walton, which are named with Old English walh ‘foreigner, Briton, serf’ (genitive plural wala), wald ‘woodland’, wall ‘wall’, or wælla ‘well, spring, stream’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

History: George Walton (1741–1804) signed the Declaration of Independence. He was born in Prince Edward County, VA, whither his grandfather had emigrated from England in 1682. He moved to Savannah, GA, and became governor of GA and a prominent jurist.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John de Walton was born about 1316, in Wivenhoe, Essex, England, his father, Sir Thomas Walton, was 25 and his mother, Elizabeth Aspall the Elder, was 24. He married Margaret de Sutton in 1339, in Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1393, in Great Staughton, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 78.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John de Waltonが約1316年にイギリスエセックスウィベンhオで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Waltonは25歳，母Elizabeth Aspall the Elderは24歳であった。1339年にイギリスイギリスエセックスでMargaret de Suttonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1393年にイギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアグレアト スタウトンで78歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Walton(이)가 대략 1316년 영국 에세스 위븐호에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas Walton(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Aspall the Elder(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1339년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 Margaret de Sutton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1393년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 그리트 스타그h턴에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он умер в 1393 г. в местности грит стотон, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 78 лет.
